title: Invariant
description: A non-negotiable property that must hold at all times across the system's lifetime. IDs follow the INV- scheme.

statement.description: The invariant, phrased as a property that is always true (e.g. 'no PII is written to logs').

rationale.description: Why this must never be violated.

source.description: Provenance: quoted span or input section.

enforcement.description: How the invariant is enforced or checked (mechanism, test, gate).

scope.description: Whether the invariant applies to the MVP or the full target.
